faq.question: How much does window replacement cost in San Francisco?
faq.answer: San Francisco homeowners typically invest $9,000 to $25,000 for a full window replacement project. The city's large stock of Victorian, Edwardian, and Craftsman homes often requires custom-sized sash and specialty glazing to match original profiles. Neighborhoods like Pacific Heights and St. Francis Wood — where homes sell for $3–5+ million — regularly see projects in the $25,000–$50,000 range. Oceanside neighborhoods in the Outer Sunset and Outer Richmond demand marine-grade specifications. Single window installation starts at approximately $750–$900.

faq.question: Do I need a permit for window replacement in San Francisco?
faq.answer: Yes. San Francisco requires a building permit for virtually all window replacements. Properties in historic districts — including Alamo Square, Mission Dolores, and Pacific Heights — may also need Planning Department approval to ensure compatibility with the character of the historic district. The SF permitting process is thorough; our team manages all applications, Planning Department submittals, and inspection scheduling on your behalf.

faq.question: Which window brands does Deluxe Windows install in San Francisco?
faq.answer: San Francisco's architectural and environmental demands call for the best. We most frequently install Marvin Integrity fiberglass-clad for Victorian and Edwardian homes needing superior moisture resistance, Andersen 400-Series for period-appropriate wood interiors, and Western Window Systems for contemporary homes in the Mission, SoMa, and Mission Bay requiring large sliding glass systems.

faq.question: Does Deluxe Windows serve all San Francisco neighborhoods?
faq.answer: Yes — we serve all San Francisco neighborhoods including the Outer Sunset, Richmond, Noe Valley, Bernal Heights, Pacific Heights, Mission, Castro, Inner Sunset, Cole Valley, and all 94xxx zip codes.
